title: PromotionScore
description: Computed harness-promotion score for a single candidate (AC-877). Combines the dense quality signal with the sparse per-case success rate, marginal token cost, error rate, and score variance under a named, versioned set of weights. This is the artifact the promotion gate reads to decide whether a candidate advances. Shared source of truth for both the Python and TypeScript autocontext packages.

schema_version.description: Schema version for forward compatibility. Always 1 for this revision.

candidate_id.description: Stable unique identifier of the candidate this score belongs to.

weight_version.description: Version tag of the weight set used to compute this score.

dense_quality_score.description: Dense quality signal, typically a judge or rubric score.

sparse_success_rate.description: Fraction of target cases the candidate resolved, in [0, 1].

tokens_per_million.description: Marginal token cost expressed per million tokens.

error_rate.description: Fraction of runs that errored, in [0, 1].

score_variance.description: Variance of the score across samples.

components.description: The measured components that feed the weighted promotion score.

sparse_success_weight.description: Weight applied to the sparse success rate.

token_cost_weight.description: Weight applied to the marginal token cost.

error_weight.description: Weight applied to the error rate.

variance_weight.description: Weight applied to the score variance.

weights.description: The named weights applied to each component.

python.description: Implementation status of this candidate in the Python package.

typescript.description: Implementation status of this candidate in the TypeScript package.

schema_hash.description: Content hash of the shared schema the two implementations agree on.

parity.description: Cross-language parity status for this candidate.
